title: caesalpinia glabra
intro: Caesalpinia glabra, also known as the rough powderpuff or small powderpuff, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's health benefits include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Caesalpinia glabra can be used to make a tea or infusion, while the roots can be used to create a decoction for various medicinal purposes.
